zh.Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England: 英國武斯特斯爾吉德米斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Urian St. Pierre於1277年5月19日出生在英國爾貝斯頓時，他的父親Sir John de Saint Pierre21歲，母親Katherine Dutton18歲。他於1308年與Iseult de Grey結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1311年3月26日之前在英國爾。

zh-hans.Kidderminster, Worcestershire, England: 英国武斯特斯爾吉德米斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Urian St. Pierre于1277年5月19日出生在英国爾貝斯頓时，他的父亲Sir John de Saint Pierre21岁，母亲Katherine Dutton18岁。他于1308年与Iseult de Grey结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1311年3月26日之前在英国爾。

nameMeanings.name: St.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: St
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: Pierre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jacques, Antoine, Yves, Michel, Jean Claude, Mireille, Jean Robert, Francois, Monique, Raoul, Serge.
French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian:

from the personal name Pierre, French form of Peter (see also below). This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Jean and Joseph ) and in Saint Martin. It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in Trinidad and Tobago, Saint Lucia, Grenada, and Bahamas).

variants.name: Perrault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Gisele, Jacques, Marcel, Roch, Alphonse, Andre, Donat, Emile, Francoise, Gaston, Ghislaine.

French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Paro , Parro , Parrow , Pearo , Perault , Pero , Perreault , Perro , Perrot , and Perrow .

History: Jacques Perrault/Perrot dit Villedaigre from Mons in Charente-Maritime, France, married Michelle Le Flot in Quebec City, QC, in 1654. Nicolas Perrault/Perrot from Darcey in Côte-d’Or married Madeleine Raclos in QC in 1671. Pierre Perrault from France married Geneviève Duclos in QC c. 1686. François Perrault from Cosne-Cours-sur-Loire in Nièvre married Jeanne-Suzanne Pagé in Quebec City, QC, in 1715. — In North America, this surname was originally (also) a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Louis Leparon dit Perrault (a son of Pierre Leparon from Derval in Loire-Atlantique, France), who married Marie-Josèphe Normandeau in Quebec City, QC, in 1751. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered forms Perrow and Pero) and also in the similar registers of the Huguenot Society of America and of the Huguenot Society of the Founders of Manakin in the Colony of Virginia. The Huguenot ancestor was Charles Perrault from Bordeaux in France, one of the settlers of Manakin, VA, in the early 1700s. However, his descendants bear only altered forms of the surname, such as Perrow and Perow.

variants.name: Perot
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse, Antoine, Philippe, Remy.
French (Pérot): from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Perrow .
Altered form of French   Perret  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Lapierre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Lucien, Normand, Adrien, Alcide, Aristide, Arnaud, Julien, Pierre, Aime.
French, Walloon, and Flemish:

topographic name, with fused French feminine definite article la, for someone who lived on a patch of stony soil or by a large outcrop of rock, from Old French pierre ‘stone, rock’ (compare Pierre 2); or a habitational name from any of various places called Lapierre or La Pierre in France and Belgium (Wallonia), with the same meaning.

variants.name: Paret
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Philippe, Pierre.  Spanish Joaquin, Alberto, Caridad, Eduardo, Eugenio, Lourdes, Patricio, Wilfredo.
French: habitational name from (Le) Paret, the name of several places in various parts of France, ultimately derived from Latin paries, genitive parietis ‘wall’ (compare 3 below).
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Pierre  .

variants.name: Peregoy
meanings: Americanized form of French Périgord: habitational name from Périgord, the name of a former province in France, of Gaulish origin.

Alternatively, an Americanized form of northwestern French Périgois, which may possibly be a cognate of 1 above, or, more likely, a regional derivative of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Pedigo , Perigo , Perrigo , and Pherigo .

History: The progenitor of the Peregoys was Joseph Peregoy, a Frenchman who came to Baltimore County, MD, in 1685. In North America his surname was first recorded as Peregois and later spelled in many other ways, including Perigo, Perigoe, and Perrigo. The majority of his descendants (through his son Henry) bear the surname Pedigo .

variants.name: Pearre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre.
Possibly also an altered form of French   Pierre  .
Altered form of English   Perry  .

variants.name: Peron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cecile, Fernand, Herve, Michel, Philippe, Virginie.
French (Péron): from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter . Compare Perron 1 and 2.
Breton (Péron, rarely Le Péron): variant of Pédron (see Pedron ).

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

variants.name: Perret
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Emile, Jacques, Michel, Andree, Dominique, Etienne, Fernand, Henri, Solange.
French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Perre, a variant of Pierre , French form of Peter .
English (Somerset, Gloucestershire and Wiltshire): variant of   Parrott  .

variants.name: Patriquin
meanings: Altered form of French Petrequin: from a pet form of Petre, a regional variant of the personal name Pierre ‘Peter’.

nameMeanings.name: Urian
meanings: Danish: A husbandman

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Urian St. Pierre was born on 19 May 1277, in Beeston, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir John de Saint Pierre, was 21 and his mother, Katherine Dutton, was 18. He married Iseult de Grey in 1308. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died before 26 March 1311, in Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Urian St. Pierre est né le 19 mai 1277, à Beeston, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Saint Pierre, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Katherine Dutton, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Iseult de Grey en 1308. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé avant 26 mars 1311, à Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Urian St. Pierre am 19. Mai 1277 in Beeston, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de Saint Pierre 21 und seine Mutter Katherine Dutton 18. Er hat 1308 Iseult de Grey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb vor 26. März 1311 in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Urian St. Pierre è nato il il 19 maggio 1277 nel Beeston, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John de Saint Pierre, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Katherine Dutton, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Iseult de Grey nel 1308. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì prima del 26 marzo 1311, nel Cheshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Urian St. Pierre(이)가 1277년 5월 19일 영국 체실르 브이스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de Saint Pierre(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Dutton(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1308년 Iseult de Grey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1311년 3월 26일 이전 영국 체실르에서 사망했다.

pt.19 May 1277–before 26 March 1311: 19 de maio de 1277–antes 26 de março de 1311
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Urian St. Pierre nasceu em 19 de maio de 1277, em Beeston, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Saint Pierre, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Dutton, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Iseult de Grey em 1308. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu antes 26 de março de 1311, em Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Urian St. Pierre родился 19 мая 1277 г. в местности бистон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John de Saint Pierre было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Katherine Dutton, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Iseult de Grey в 1308 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер перед 26 марта 1311 г. в местности чешир, Англия.
